records.ety: [L. inseparabilis: cf. F. insparable. See In-, and Separable.]

defs: Not separable; incapable of being separated or disjoined.
quotes: The history of every language is inseparable from that of the people by whom it is spoken.
Liberty and union, now and forever, one and inseparable.

defs: Invariably attached to some word, stem, or root; as, the inseparable particle un-.
